title: Minimum Moves to Make Array Complementary
titleCn: 使数组互补的最少操作次数

testcases.input: nums = [1,2,4,3], limit = 4 Output: 1 Explanation: In 1 move, you can change nums to [1,2,2,3] (underlined elements are changed). nums[0] + nums[3] = 1 + 3 = 4. nums[1] + nums[2] = 2 + 2 = 4. nums[2] + nums[1] = 2 + 2 = 4. nums[3] + nums[0] = 3 + 1 = 4. Therefore, nums[i] + nums[n-1-i] = 4 for every i, so nums is complementary.
testcases.expectedOutput: 1 Explanation: In 1 move, you can change nums to [1,2,2,3] (underlined elements are changed). nums[0] + nums[3] = 1 + 3 = 4. nums[1] + nums[2] = 2 + 2 = 4. nums[2] + nums[1] = 2 + 2 = 4. nums[3] + nums[0] = 3 + 1 = 4. Therefore, nums[i] + nums[n-1-i] = 4 for every i, so nums is complementary.

testcases.input: nums = [1,2,2,1], limit = 2 Output: 2 Explanation: In 2 moves, you can change nums to [2,2,2,2]. You cannot change any number to 3 since 3 > limit.
testcases.expectedOutput: 2 Explanation: In 2 moves, you can change nums to [2,2,2,2]. You cannot change any number to 3 since 3 > limit.

testcases.input: nums = [1,2,1,2], limit = 2 Output: 0 Explanation: nums is already complementary.
